name: Linux Host Firewall Control (iptables)
description: Comprehensive host-based firewall control leveraging Linux iptables to enforce packet filtering, connection tracking, and network segmentation policies on Linux servers and workstations. This control defines a default-deny posture for inbound and outbound traffic, integrates logging and monitoring, and includes rule management processes to ensure consistency with organizational security policies and standards (e.g., CIS Benchmarks, NIST SP 800-41).

name: AWS Certificate Manager TLS Lifecycle Management Control
description: Centralized management of SSL/TLS certificates for AWS-hosted applications and services using AWS Certificate Manager (ACM). This control automates certificate issuance, renewal, deployment, rotation, and revocation, enforces strong TLS configuration policies, integrates with AWS CloudWatch and Certificate Transparency logs for continuous monitoring, and applies least-privilege IAM policies to restrict certificate management operations. Aligned with AWS Well-Architected Framework, CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark, NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2, and ISO/IEC 27001 A.10 cryptographic controls, it ensures end-to-end protection of data in transit and rapid response to certificate compromise.

issuance, validation, and renewal of SSL/TLS certificates via AWS Certificate Manager for integrated AWS resources."}, "auto_managed_resource_types": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "enable_certificate_renewal_alerts": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Certificate Renewal Alerts", "description": "Enable CloudWatch Events alerts on ACM certificate issuance or renewal failures."}, "certificate_renewal_cloudwatch_event_rule_name": {"type": "string", "title": "Certificate Renewal Event Rule Name", "description": "Name of the CloudWatch Events rule monitoring ACM certificate issuance and renewal events."}, "enable_certificate_renewal_retry_function": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Certificate Renewal Retry Function", "description": "Enable Lambda "enable_securityhub_integration": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Security Hub Integration", "description": "Enable ingestion of ACM certificate findings into AWS Security Hub for centralized name: Rate Limiting Controls
description: A set of technical controls designed to prevent service degradation, denial-of-service attacks, brute-force attempts, and API abuse by enforcing request rate limits across web servers, API gateways, and application endpoints. This control defines mechanisms for per-client, per-user, and global rate thresholds with configurable policies at the network edge or application layer. It aligns with OWASP ASVS v4.0.3, NIST SP 800-53 SC-5 (Denial of Service Protection), and implements defense-in-depth by combining preventive enforcement, real-time monitoring, and automated isolation of abusive clients.

name: PCI DSS Payment Processing Control
description: A comprehensive control to ensure PCI DSS Level 1 compliance for payment processing services. Encompasses end-to-end encryption with Hardware Security Module (HSM) integration for key management, credit card tokenization, strict segmentation of the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE), role-based access control with multi-factor authentication, automated compliance monitoring and reporting, tamper-evident audit trail generation, real-time fraud detection and anomaly monitoring, and defined incident response procedures. Applicable in on-premises, cloud, or hybrid architectures handling payment data.

name: System Operators Security Training Requirements
description: An administrative control mandating a structured training program for system operators to ensure understanding and application of general security requirements and incident response procedures. Based on ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.7.2 terminology, this control covers foundational security principles, secure configuration management, access control best practices, and hands-on incident response exercises to strengthen organizational resilience and readiness.

name: TLS Configuration Hardening
description: Ensures that web servers and TLS-enabled components enforce strong, up-to-date encryption settings by mandating a minimum protocol version, disabling weak or legacy cipher suites, enforcing forward secrecy, and deploying HTTP Strict Transport Security. Provides continuous validation and automated certificate lifecycle management to reduce the risk of protocol downgrade, cipher attacks, and certificate expiration. Aligns with NIST SP 800-52 Rev. 2, CIS TLS Benchmark, and OWASP ASVS recommendations.
template: {"schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"minimum_tls_version": {"type": "string", "enum": ["TLS1.2", "TLS1.3"], "title": "Minimum TLS Version", "description": "Specifies the minimum allowed TLS protocol version for connections."}, "prefer_tls_13": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Prefer TLS 1.3", "description": "Prefer TLS 1.3 for connections when supported by clients."}, "disable_legacy_ciphers": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Disable Legacy Ciphers", "description": "Disable all weak or legacy cipher suites in the server cipher list."}, "cipher_disallowed_list": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Cipher Disallowed List", "description": "List of cipher suite identifiers to explicitly block."}, "cipher_allowed_list": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Cipher Allowed List", "description": "Ordered list of approved cipher suites that support AEAD and forward secrecy."}, "require_forward_secrecy": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Require Forward Secrecy", "description": "Require use of cipher suites that provide forward secrecy."}, "hsts_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable HSTS", "description": "Enable sending of HTTP Strict Transport Security headers on all HTTPS responses."}, "hsts_max_age_seconds": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 63072000, "title": "HSTS Max Age (Seconds)", "description": "Max-age directive for HSTS headers in seconds."}, "hsts_include_subdomains": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Include Subdomains in HSTS", "description": "IncludeSubDomains directive for HSTS to cover all subdomains."}, "hsts_preload": {"type": "boolean", "title": "HSTS Preload", "description": "Preload directive for HSTS to submit the site to browser preload lists."}, "hsts_target_domains": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "HSTS Target Domains", "description": "List of domains or hostnames that must include the HSTS header."}, "require_hsts_header": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Require HSTS Header", "description": "Require HTTP Strict Transport Security header to be present during compliance scans."}, "hsts_header_inspection_interval_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 168, "title": "HSTS Header Inspection Interval (Hours)", "description": "Interval in hours between automated HSTS header inspection tests."}, "hsts_misconfiguration_alert_threshold_per_day": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 100, "title": "HSTS Misconfiguration Alert Threshold (Per Day)", "description": "Allowed number of HSTS header misconfigurations per day before alerting."}, "scan_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable TLS Scanning", "description": "Enable periodic TLS endpoint scanning for unsupported protocols, weak ciphers, expired certificates, and missing HSTS."}, "scan_frequency_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 168, "title": "TLS Scan Frequency (Hours)", "description": "Frequency in hours at which TLS endpoints are scanned."}, "scan_tools": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "TLS Scan Tools", "description": "List of scanning tools or methods to detect TLS misconfiguration issues."}, "scan_endpoints": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "TLS Scan Endpoints", "description": "List of TLS-enabled endpoints to be scanned and monitored."}, "siem_integration_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "SIEM Integration Enabled", "description": "Enable forwarding of scan results to SIEM or vulnerability management platform."}, "remediation_ticket_sla_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 168, "title": "Remediation Ticket SLA (Hours)", "description": "Maximum allowed time in hours to create remediation tickets for scan failures."}, "certificate_automation": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Certificate Automation", "description": "Enable automated certificate lifecycle management, issuance, renewal, and revocation."}, "certificate_automation_method": {"type": "string", "enum": ["ACME", "PKI"], "title": "Certificate Automation Method", "description": "Select the automation method for certificate provisioning and renewal."}, "certificate_renewal_threshold_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 90, "title": "Certificate Renewal Threshold (Days)", "description": "Number of days before certificate expiration to trigger automated renewal."}, "certificate_validation_interval_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 168, "title": "Certificate Validation Interval (Hours)", "description": "Interval in hours to revalidate certificate status."}, "max_certificate_validity_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 365, "title": "Max Certificate Validity (Days)", "description": "Maximum allowed validity period for issued certificates."}, "allowed_certificate_authorities": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Allowed Certificate Authorities", "description": "List of approved certificate authorities for issuance and renewal."}, "ocsp_stapling_enabled": {"type": "boolean", name: PCI-DSS Compliance Monitoring Control
description: Continuous and periodic monitoring control to ensure that applications, databases, network devices, and other hybrid-environment components maintain compliance with PCI-DSS requirements. This control collects, analyzes, and reports on configuration, access, and event data to detect deviations, support remediation workflows, and provide audit-ready evidence across on-premises and cloud systems.

name: Web Server Access Control Framework
description: This control establishes an integrated access control framework for web server components, ensuring only authenticated and authorized users or services can access web application resources and administrative interfaces. It applies to all web servers across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments. The framework encompasses role-based access control, multi-factor authentication, secure session management, network-level filtering, and continuous monitoring. Aligns with NIST SP 800-53 AC controls and MITRE D3FEND tactics to mitigate unauthorized access, privilege escalation, and session hijacking threats.

name: eCommerce Security Baseline Control
description: A comprehensive security foundation for online retail platforms handling customer data and payment processing. Establishes minimum security requirements across web applications, APIs, databases, and external integrations. Addresses payment fraud, data breaches, account takeover, and ensures compliance with PCI DSS, OWASP Top 10, ISO 27001, and NIST SP 800-53 standards.

name: PII Data Privacy Protection Control
description: Comprehensive control to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and lawful processing of personally identifiable information (PII) across its entire lifecycle. This control enforces data minimization, purpose limitation, retention policies, and user consent management in accordance with GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations. It applies to all customer PII—names, addresses, emails, purchase history—whether at rest, in transit, or in use, and integrates with data discovery, classification, access management, encryption, logging, and data lifecycle systems.

name: PCI DSS Compliance Framework
description: Comprehensive control framework for systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data in an eCommerce environment. Ensures alignment with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) requirements by enforcing strong encryption, strict access controls, network segmentation, continuous monitoring, and robust vulnerability management. Designed to protect cardholder data at rest, in transit, and during processing while providing measurable compliance and operational visibility.

name: API Gateway Security Control
description: Comprehensive security control for API gateways that manage both external and internal API traffic. This control enforces rate limiting, validates request payloads and schemas, enforces strong authentication and authorization, secures API versioning, and protects against common API threats such as injection, broken object-level authorization, and credential abuse. It integrates preventive, detective, and isolating measures suitable for cloud, on-premises, or hybrid deployments and aligns with industry standards for resilient API security.

name: Managed Database Security Control
description: Comprehensive security control for managed database services handling sensitive business data. Enforces data confidentiality, integrity, and availability by requiring strong encryption (at rest and in transit), strict access controls with least-privilege, continuous activity monitoring and anomaly detection, secure backup and recovery procedures, proactive injection prevention, and network-level exfiltration safeguards. Designed for cloud-hosted or hybrid managed database environments, aligned with NIST SP 800-53 and CIS Benchmarks.

name: AWS Lambda Function Security Control
description: A comprehensive security control for AWS Lambda functions designed to prevent function compromise, credential leakage, and unauthorized access to backend resources. This control enforces least-privilege IAM permissions, encrypts and restricts access to environment variables, isolates functions within VPCs, integrates automated secrets management, and implements runtime monitoring and anomaly detection. Aligned with AWS Well-Architected Serverless best practices and MITRE D3FEND, it delivers defense-in-depth for serverless compute.

"description": "List of permitted IAM actions that Lambda functions may perform."}, "iam_allowed_resource_scope": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Allowed IAM Resource Scope", "description": "List of resource ARNs or wildcards that Lambda roles are allowed to access."}, "iam_access_analyzer_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable IAM Access Analyzer", "description": "Enable AWS IAM Access Analyzer scans for identifying overly permissive policies."}, "iam_access_analyzer_schedule_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 168, "title": "Access Analyzer Schedule (Hours)", "description": "Frequency, in hours, at which Access Analyzer scans are executed."}, "iam_auto_remediation_unused_permissions": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Auto-Remediate Unused "minimum": 1, "maximum": 100, "title": "Policy Violation Alert Threshold", "description": "Number of detected policy violations required to trigger an alert."}, "env_var_encryption_enabled": {"type": "env_var_enforce_encryption_in_transit": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enforce Encryption In Transit", "description": "Ensure that environment variables are transmitted encrypted within Lambda and to name: ISO 27001 A.5.1 Information Security Policies and Procedures Framework
description: Establishes a comprehensive information security governance framework for the eCommerce organization by defining, approving, communicating, reviewing, and monitoring all information security policies and procedures. Covers policy development lifecycle (risk-based analysis, drafting, management approval), assignment of roles and responsibilities, mandatory policy distribution and acknowledgment, ongoing security awareness training, and scheduled reviews with compliance audits to ensure policies remain current and effective across all data flows, stores, and processing components.
template: {"schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"policy_framework_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Unified Information Security Policy Framework", "description": "Enable unified information security policy framework establishment, including risk assessment, policy domain definition, and executive approval."}, "policy_domains": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}, "title": "Policy Domains", "description": "List of policy domains defined in the information security policy framework (e.g., data classification, access control, incident management)."}, "risk_assessment_frequency_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 365, "title": "Risk Assessment Frequency (Days)", "description": "Interval in days for conducting formal operational risk assessments to identify required policy domains."}, "roles_responsibilities_assignment_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Roles & Responsibilities Assignment", "description": "Enable assignment of clear roles and responsibilities for information security policy governance."}, "policy_management_system_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Policy Management System", "description": "Enable centralized policy distribution and acknowledgement system."}, "training_program_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Security Training Program", "description": name: ISO 27001 A.5.7 Information Security Risk Management Control
description: Establishes a systematic risk management process for the eCommerce platform’s data flows and supporting infrastructure. Scope includes all application components, data stores, interfaces, and network segments involved in order processing, payment, and customer data management. The control mandates: • Threat modeling using data flow diagrams (DFDs) to identify and analyze threats to each component. • A centralized, versioned risk register capturing identified risks, likelihood, impact, and risk owners. • Risk treatment plans specifying mitigating controls, implementation timelines, and residual risk acceptance criteria. • Automated monitoring of key risk indicators and integration with SIEM for anomaly detection. • Quarterly reassessment and audit to validate control effectiveness and update risk profiles. Aligned to ISO 27001 A.5.7 and leveraging best practices in threat modeling and risk treatment to ensure continuous protection of critical eCommerce assets.

name: Supplier Relationship Security Management
description: A comprehensive control to manage and mitigate security risks arising from third-party suppliers and service providers in accordance with ISO/IEC 27001 A.5.19–A.5.21. This control ensures that all critical external dependencies—such as payment gateways, email services, and cloud infrastructure—are subject to rigorous security assessment, contractually enforced security requirements, and continuous monitoring. It covers the entire supplier lifecycle from selection through offboarding, integrating risk-based processes, technical enforcement, and ongoing governance.

name: Integrated Security in Project Management Control (ISO 27001 A.5.8)
description: Ensures that security requirements are systematically integrated into project management and the eCommerce platform development lifecycle. This control covers defining and managing security requirements, conducting security architecture reviews, embedding secure development practices, and performing security testing throughout design, build, and deployment phases. It aligns with ISO 27001 A.5.8 and establishes governance, technical processes, and checkpoints to reduce vulnerabilities, enforce best practices, and ensure traceable security decision-making.
template: {"schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"security_requirements_management_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Security Requirements Management", "description": "Enable systematic integration of security requirements into the project management and development lifecycle."}, "requirements_tool_integration_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Requirements "string"}, "title": "Security Requirements Phases", "description": "Project lifecycle phases where security requirements must be defined or updated (e.g., initiation, design, build, deployment)."}, "update_frequency_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 365, "title": "Security Requirements Review Frequency (Days)", "description": "Maximum number of days between successive reviews or updates of security requirements."}, "approval_timeout_hours": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 72, "title": "Approval Timeout (Hours)", "description": "Maximum hours allowed for stakeholders to approve or reject security requirements at each phase gate."}, "manual_process_documented": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Documented Manual Process", "description": "Indicates whether "Manual Review Interval (Days)", "description": "Interval in days for manual periodic review of security requirements."}, "audit_schedule_defined": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Audit Schedule Defined", "description": "Indicates if an audit schedule for verifying adherence to documented security requirements is defined."}, "architecture_review_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable "boolean", "title": "Require Reviewer Signoff", "description": "Require documented signoff from architecture reviewers before progressing to the next phase."}, "remediation_tracking_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Remediation Tracking", "description": "Enable tracking and verification of remediation actions for review findings."}, "remediation_deadline_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 90, "title": "Remediation Deadline (Days)", "description": "Maximum allowed days to remediate documented findings before escalation."}, "automated_scan_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable Automated Architecture Scans", "description": "Enable automated design and vulnerability scanning as part of the architecture review."}, "automated_scan_frequency_days": {"type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 365, "title": "Automated Scan Frequency (Days)", "description": "Interval in days between automated architecture scans."}, "sast_enabled": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Enable SAST Integration", "description": "Enable Static Application Security Testing (SAST) integration in the CI/CD pipeline."}, "sast_tool": {"type": "string", "title": "SAST Tool", "description": "Name of the SAST tool to run (e.g., SonarQube, Coverity, Veracode)."}, "sast_scan_on_merge_request": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Run SAST on Merge Requests", "description": "Execute SAST scan automatically on merge requests."}, "fail_on_critical": {"type": "boolean", "title": "Fail on Critical SAST Issues", "description": "Fail the CI/CD pipeline if any critical severity name: eCommerce Platform Security Awareness and Training Program
description: Comprehensive, role-based security education program for all personnel involved with the eCommerce platform. Aligned to ISO 27001 A.6.3, the program covers organizational security responsibilities, threat awareness, secure coding best practices, and formal incident reporting procedures. Training is tailored by role (developers, operations, business users), delivered via an integrated e-learning platform, enforced through access controls, and measured through ongoing assessments, simulations, and feedback loops to ensure sustained, measurable improvement in security posture.
